packet.criterion_text: Objective severity trend: PASI, BSA, and/or IGA show a directional change across visits.
packet.reasoning: PASI rose from 6.1 at baseline (2026-02-02) to 14.2 at V3 (2026-05-10) and 16.8 at V4 (2026-06-29); BSA rose 8% to 18% and IGA stepped from 2 to 3 over the same interval. Objective severity is worsening on all three indices.

packet.criterion_text: Symptom trajectory, including nail and joint involvement, shows a directional change.

evidence.quote: new scalp plaques along the posterior hairline
worse over the past few weeks

packet.reasoning: New nail findings (onycholysis) and new scalp plaques appeared at V3, and the patient reports subjective worsening in the V4-interval portal message. Symptom trajectory is worsening. Note: the nail finding was documented only in the nursing note; the derm notes never use the phrase 'nail involvement'.

packet.criterion_text: DLQI (Dermatology Life Quality Index) trend shows a directional change.
packet.reasoning: DLQI rose from 7 to 15 to 17, a sustained increase well beyond the 4-point threshold. Quality-of-life impact is worsening.

packet.criterion_text: Treatment-response adequacy: the current regimen has been given an adequate, adherent trial.

evidence.quote: start adalimumab 40 mg subcutaneously every 2 weeks, first dose administered today
doses taken 2026-05-10 and 2026-05-24; doses due 2026-06-07 and 2026-06-21 were missed

packet.reasoning: Adalimumab was started 2026-05-10 at 40 mg q2wk, but two of the four scheduled doses were missed (2026-06-07, 2026-06-21), plus a 3-week NB-UVB gap. The regimen has not received an adequate adherent trial, so response adequacy cannot be assessed.

packet.criterion_text: Worsening cannot be attributed to disease if a confounder (non-adherence, infection, steroid-withdrawal or seasonal flare) is unaddressed.

evidence.quote: worse over the past few weeks

packet.reasoning: CONCEDED after round-1 challenge. A non-adherence confounder (two missed adalimumab doses) is present and unaddressed: the chart never establishes whether flare onset preceded the first missed dose (2026-06-07), and no clinician note attributes the worsening independent of the missed doses. Documentation that would settle it: a dated symptom-onset earlier than 2026-06-07, or an explicit clinical assessment excluding non-adherence as the driver.

challenges.challenge_reason: Packet item 1 asserts objective worsening, while packet item 5's own evidence documents two missed adalimumab doses with no exclusion of non-adherence as the cause of the decline. The packet is internally inconsistent: it attributes worsening to disease while an unexcluded confounder sits in its own evidence.
challenges.rubric_quote: Worsening cannot be attributed to disease if a confounder (non-adherence, infection, steroid-withdrawal or seasonal flare) is unaddressed.
challenges.what_would_satisfy: Packet evidence that flare onset predates the first missed dose (2026-06-07), or a chart statement excluding non-adherence as the driver of the worsening.
